summary: High‑energy Alligator jam, tight dual drums, fiery Lovelight
blurb: The show bursts open with a scorching Turn On Your Lovelight and rolls into an incendiary Alligator → Caution sequence. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Mickey and Bill drive an intense, incredible groove. Highlights also include The Other One, Feedback and a heartfelt And We Bid You Good Night.
review: December 21, 1968 at the Shrine Exposition Hall captured one of the Dead’s most ferocious late‑'60s outings. From the opening “Turn On Your Lovelight” the band is in high gear, with Jerry Garcia’s lead guitar cooking through an extended Alligator jam that segues into a thunderous Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) climax – a moment reviewers call “high‑energy” and “acid‑drenched”. The dual drumming of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann is repeatedly described as “incredible”, propelling the set forward with relentless drive. Between the jams, the band delivers a blistering rendition of “The Other One”, wild noise excursions on “Feedback”, and a soulful close with “And We Bid You Good Night”. Pigpen’s presence adds grit, while Phil Lesh’s bass underpins the chaos with solid grounding. The Miller soundboard capture (gd1968-12-21.sbd.miller.89718.sbeok.flac16) preserves the crispness and vibrancy of the performance despite a faint tape hiss, making this recording the definitive way to experience the night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are uniformly positive – reviewers repeatedly praise the high‑energy Alligator jam, intense dual drumming, and overall locked‑in performance. No negative comments about musical execution appear, resulting in a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with good, crisp audio that captures the band’s energy; most reviewers cite its vibrant sound despite minor tape hiss.

key_highlights: Alligator → Caution extended jam that drives the set
"Turn On Your Lovelight" opener bursts with fire
song_highlights: And We Bid You Good Night

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is a highlight all night – the Alligator jam ‘cooks’ and his tone shines throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid, supportive bass foundation; his playing on “And We Bid You Good Night” is specifically praised.
band_performance.Bob: Weir contributes a brief vocal cameo toward the end of the show.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is described as incredible, adding powerful texture to the dual‑drum attack.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming is equally intense, delivering a standout rhythmic drive that fuels the high‑energy jams.

must_listen_sequences: And We Bid You Good Night
